title: salix permollis
intro: Salix permollis, also known as the Dyer's Willow or Japanese Willow, is a species of willow tree native to East Asia, characterized by its small, yellow-green leaves and yellow catkins. The bark and leaves of the Salix permollis plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
